mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic strength, leading to upward revisions of corporate earnings expectations and boosting equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value of 148177.0 indicates continued job creation.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the headline inflation index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds a key psychological or policy threshold, it triggers investor expectations of aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, increasing discount rates and causing a sell-off in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest CPIAUCSL at 249.619 is approaching the trigger threshold.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL is above 250, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than the average return when CPIAUCSL is below 250.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) is bullish for equities only when the labor market is also tight (low UNRATE), as this combination confirms sustainable consumer purchasing power rather than a debt-fueled, temporary spike.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% meets the trigger condition.
failure_condition: A strategy going long S&P 500 when 3-month RSAFS growth is positive and UNRATE is below 4.5% fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a marked deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) growth serves as a leading indicator of an impending recession, as businesses curtail output in anticipation of falling demand, negatively impacting equity markets.
failure_condition: Following a negative 3-month change in INDPRO during late-cycle periods, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE), as defined by the Sahm Rule indicator, signals the onset of a recession, leading to a sharp decline in risk appetite and equity prices.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.1% is low, so trigger is not active.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict more than 2 of the last 5 NBER-defined recessions, or it generates more than 3 false positives (triggers without a subsequent recession) in a 20-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) acts as a direct input into equity valuation models. A higher policy rate increases the discount rate for future cash flows, exerting continuous downward pressure on the S&P 500 index level.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.42% is historically low, suggesting a weak (but still negative) signal effect.
failure_condition: The rolling 36-month correlation between the FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive for a sustained period (more than 12 consecutive months).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a sharp contraction in the interest-rate sensitive housing sector, creating negative spillovers to the broader economy and depressing stock market returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year change in HOUST < -5% AND Year-over-year change in FEDFUNDS > 1.0pp
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly strong monthly retail sales (RSAFS) print causes an immediate upward revision to near-term GDP and corporate revenue forecasts, leading to a positive short-term reaction in the S&P 500.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average return on non-trigger days.
